context: Tests the complete QR generation workflow: slug from spec, bitly with custom back-half (not random hash), QR encodes bitly URL (decoupling layer), and generate-qr.py used (not ad-hoc). Every failure mode from the field-test post-mortem is covered by a criterion.

checklist.name: Talk slug read from Presentation Spec
checklist.description: The talk slug '2026-04-16-devnexus-robocoders-judgment-day' is read from presentation-spec.md. The agent does not invent, abbreviate, or rephrase the slug. Any reference to --talk-slug uses this exact value

checklist.name: Shownotes URL constructed from pattern + spec slug
checklist.description: The shownotes URL is exactly 'https://jbaru.ch/2026-04-16-devnexus-robocoders-judgment-day', constructed by substituting the spec slug into the profile's shownotes_url_pattern. Not a made-up URL

checklist.name: Bitly custom back-half is the talk slug
checklist.description: The planned bitly link uses the talk slug as the custom back-half: bit.ly/2026-04-16-devnexus-robocoders-judgment-day. NOT a random hash like bit.ly/a3xK9f. The plan explicitly references the slug being used as the back-half

checklist.name: QR encodes bitly URL, not raw shownotes URL
checklist.description: The QR code is planned to encode the bitly URL (https://bit.ly/2026-04-16-devnexus-robocoders-judgment-day), not the raw shownotes URL. The plan explains the decoupling rationale: if shownotes URL changes, update bitly target, printed QR stays valid

checklist.name: generate-qr.py used, not qrcode library directly
checklist.description: The plan references generate-qr.py as the script to use. The agent does not write its own QR generation code or call the qrcode library directly. The command includes proper arguments: --talk-slug, --shownotes-url, and vault path

checklist.name: Command uses --shownotes-url (not --short-url)
checklist.description: The generate-qr.py command uses --shownotes-url (letting the script handle bitly shortening), not --short-url. The script manages the shortener API call, custom back-half, and tracking DB update

checklist.name: Tracking DB update mentioned
checklist.description: The plan acknowledges that generate-qr.py will update the tracking database with qr_codes[] metadata including the talk slug, bitly URL, and link ID

checklist.name: No silent degradation to raw URL
checklist.description: The plan does not fall back to encoding the raw shownotes URL. Since shortener is configured as 'bitly' and secrets.json has the API token, there is no reason to fall back. Any mention of raw URL fallback without justification = zero points
